text:
In this paper, the joint optimization of the radio and computational resources in a MIMO multicell MEC system is investigated. In today’s mobile networks, resource demanding applications are processed at the mobile devices. This results in huge energy consumption at mobile devices. The main goal of MEC is to offload the tasks from resource constrained mobile devices to the cloud servers located at the edge of the network. Therefore, the total energy consumption of all the mobile users is minimized under the constraints of latency and transmit power. This means finding the optimal precoding matrices for the users and the optimal CPU cycle provided by the cloud.

The resulting optimization problem is non-convex in both the objective and the constraints. Although this fact makes the problem very difficult to analyze, we are able to transform the single-user problem and obtain an exact global solution in closed form. Then, the multiuser case is tackled by means of an iterative algorithm based on the use of Successive Convex Approximation (SCA) methodology. The optimality of the algorithm is proved, in the sense that it converges to a local optimum of the non-convex problem. In addition, this optimization framework is highly adequate for distributed/parallel implementation over the distributed Radio Access Points (RAPs), as it only requires low amount of centralized cloud exchanges. The performance benefits of the proposed joint optimization resource allocation framework is validated by means of extensive numerical evaluations, where the proposed approach is shown to provide one order of magnitude better energy efficiency than disjoint resource allocation approaches, which is highly desirable for emerging energy-aware mobile applications.

In this paper, the joint optimization of the radio and computational resources in a MIMO multicell MEC system is investigated. In today’s mobile networks, resource demanding applications are processed at the mobile devices. This results in huge energy consumption at mobile devices. The main goal of MEC is to offload the tasks from resource constrained mobile devices to the cloud servers located at the edge of the network. Therefore, the total energy consumption of all the mobile users is minimized under the constraints of latency and transmit power.
